questions.question: Which of the following is NOT an epigraphic record for the reign of King Harsha?
Which archaeological site shows pit dwellings and evidence of domesticated dogs during the Neolithic period?
Which foreign trade relation was badly affected when Byzantium learned silk production in the 6ᵗʰ century CE?
Which Buddhist scripture explicitly states “All dhammas are without self,” affirming the doctrine of Anatta?
Which of the following substances constitutes the primary material of the "Mother Goddess" sculpture originating from the Indus Valley Civilisation?
Which of the following was NOT a kingdom based on the modern-day Tamil Nadu?
The village headman during the Gupta period was commonly known as _____.
During the Mauryan period, the village headman in North India was known by ______.
The Priest-King statue from Mohenjo-daro, a famous stone sculpture, is predominantly made of which of the following substances?
Bori, which is considered to be the first Lower Palaeolithic site, is in which of the following states of India?
The Lower Palaeolithic period in India, including the Acheulian phase, spans approximately how many years BP?
Which of the following officers managed stores and the supply of weapons like spears and swords during the Gupta period?
Social life in the Vedic period was divided into how many ashramas?
The indigenous inhabitants of India who were hostile, dark skinned and did not follow the Vedic system are referred to in Vedic texts as:
Which of the following statements about succession in the Gupta dynasty is true?
questions.option_1: Only the eldest son could become king
questions.option_2: The ruler always declared himself divine to get the throne
questions.option_3: The eldest son was always crowned Yuvraj at priority
questions.option_4: Sons had to prove their eligibility before an assembly

questions.question: Which key urban feature of Indus Valley cities shows advanced planning with streets intersecting at right angles and covered drains?
Arthashastra, which formed the basis of governance and administration during the Mauryan period, discussed which of the following problems of the citizens?
questions.option_4: Ethical and morality issues of the rulers

questions.question: Which of the following techniques was used by the Harappans to create bronze sculptures?
What was the Mauryan administrative head of city governance called, as mentioned in the Arthashastra?
In which of the following states of India are the Rock Shelters of Bhimbetka located?
The Bhimbetka rock shelters are associated with which prehistoric period?
